Fracture
qa_matrix.captured_response: Yeah, I want to share one thing, last year, eight months back I met with an accident. There was a fracture to my left hand. So I, there was a surgery, it went, and I, they put a rod on my hand. So I want to show that only.

qa_matrix.question_text: Are you awaiting any of the following for this condition: medical tests or investigations, test results or referral to hospital or surgery?
qa_matrix.captured_response: I have everything, yeah. No worries.

qa_matrix.question_text: Please provide full details of your condition including symptoms, treatment (including any surgery) and complications if any.
qa_matrix.captured_response: So actually I went through, I sent to my insurance team, so I claimed total insurance regarding this accident and surgery. So this went through all the after the investigation and like that. ... Yeah, last year May, in the May, May last week. ... May 29th actually. Yeah, May 29th.
qa_matrix.expected_response: fracture in left hand in 2025 may, surgery was done. and plate is still present.

qa_matrix.question_text: Blindness,cataracts, glaucoma, impaired sight, double vision or any other diseases or disorders of eyes?
Deafness, Impaired hearing, speech problem, hoarseness of voice, difficult in swal-lowing, ear discharge or any other diseases or disorders of ears, nose or throat?

Is there anything else that you would like to share with us with respect to your health/ habits or anything else that you believe is important to ensure that policy is processed basis all facts & information?
qa_matrix.captured_response: Yeah, I want to share one thing, last year, eight months back I met with an accident. There was a fracture to my left hand. So I, there was a surgery, it went, and I, they put a rod on my hand. So I want to show that only.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Yes, fracture in left hand, surgery done, rod still present.

qa_matrix.question_text: Other than what you have already told us, have you had a medical condition that has required any of the following within the last 5 years a)Any abnormal medical findings or pending test results, scheduled or planned or pre-scribed medical tests such as imaging scan reports (e.g. x-ray, ultrasound, MRI, PET scan, CT scan etc.), biopsy, electrocardiogram (ECG), treadmill, Mammography, Endo-scopy/Colonoscopy, PAP Smear, blood or urine test?
qa_matrix.captured_response: X-ray for hand. No, no pending results. That was already resulted and it's given and it's by doctor only. Yeah. It's completed.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Yes, X-ray for hand due to fracture.

qa_matrix.question_text: Medication or treatment that lasted more than 2 weeks or had been referred to a specialist doctor or hospital for any treatment/consultation and/or investigations (eg: Blood, ECG, MRI, Xray etc)
Inability to perform your daily activities or absent from work more than two 2 consecutive weeks at a time
qa_matrix.captured_response: Same thing. I went, I, I took for the hand only. Yeah.
qa_matrix.suggested_correction: Yes, absent from work for hand fracture.

qa_matrix.question_text: Have you suffered from any congenital defect or disorder or hereditary disease such as cerebral palsy, Down's syndrome, poliomyelitis
Have you or your spouse or partner been told to undergo tests or treatment, or received any medical advice or counselling in connection with sexually transmitted diseases, including HIV and AIDS?

Apart from what you have disclosed, are you experiencing any unexplained weight loss/gain or any sign/symptoms that you are likely to seek medical advice or treatment for or awaiting any surgical procedure.

critical_issues: Multiple discrepancies between the customer's verbal responses and the MER documentation regarding the fracture details and related medical history. The MER indicates 'No' for several questions where the customer provided 'Yes' or relevant details about their hand fracture and associated medical events.
The customer's name spelling in the MER ('Gollapelly Prashanth') differs from the name stated by the customer ('Gullapalli Prashanth').
recommendations: The doctor should ensure that all customer disclosures, especially those related to medical history, are accurately reflected in the MER. For questions where the customer provides details that contradict a 'No' in the MER, the MER should be updated to reflect the 'Yes' and the provided details.
Specifically, the MER should be updated for the fracture details, medical tests, treatment, and absence from work to align with the customer's statements.
The customer's name spelling in the MER should be verified and corrected to match the customer's stated name.

customer_satisfaction_indicators: Customer is cooperative throughout the call, despite initial technical difficulties.
Customer proactively shares important medical information (fracture).
Customer expresses gratitude at the end of the call: 'Thank you, doctor. Have a good day.'

validation_errors: Customer name spelling discrepancy: Transcript 'Gullapalli Prashanth' vs MER 'Gollapelly Prashanth'.
Discrepancy for Health.k.Fracture.Awaiting: Customer implies 'No' to awaiting tests/surgery, but MER states 'Yes'.
Discrepancy for General.1: Customer disclosed fracture, but MER states 'No' for 'anything else to share'.
Discrepancy for General.2.a: Customer mentioned X-ray for hand, but MER states 'No' for abnormal findings/pending tests.
Discrepancy for General.2.b: Customer mentioned treatment for hand, but MER states 'No' for medication/treatment lasting more than 2 weeks.
Discrepancy for General.2.c: Customer mentioned absence from work for hand, but MER states 'No' for inability to perform daily activities/absent from work.

video_analysis.attire_explanation: The doctor is wearing a white clinical apron/coat, visible in all frames.
video_analysis.visibility_explanation: Both the doctor (right side) and the customer (left side) are clearly visible in all provided frames.
video_analysis.privacy_explanation: The doctor's background is blurred in all frames, preventing any potential privacy breaches such as visible confidential data or identifiable individuals.

greetings.explanation: Doctors initiated both calls with greetings.

call_opening.explanation: Both doctors introduced themselves, confirmed the client's name, and stated the insurer's name.

language_preference.explanation: The doctor did not ask for the customer's language preference in either call.

id_validation.explanation: Initial ID validation in Call 1 failed due to blurriness, but was successfully completed in Call 2, including verification of Aadhaar digits.

disclaimer.explanation: Both doctors read out the disclaimer regarding call recording and the legal implications of the information provided.

politeness.explanation: Doctors maintained a polite tone throughout both calls, even when facing technical difficulties or customer interruptions.

empathy.explanation: The doctor showed empathy by acknowledging the customer's disclosure of a past accident and surgery, and emphasizing the importance of sharing all health information.

communication_skills.explanation: Doctors provided clear instructions and questions. However, they occasionally repeated 'Hello?' during brief silences or customer interruptions, indicating minor impatience.

probing.explanation: The doctor effectively probed for details regarding the customer's accident, fracture, and surgery, and clarified customer responses.

observations.explanation: The customer received another call during Call 1 and experienced persistent difficulty showing ID proof clearly due to blurriness/network issues.

call_closure.explanation: Call 1 ended with a reschedule due to ID issues. Call 2 concluded with a proper declaration and thank you from the doctor.
